projectName: VEGA Phase 0+1: Environment Verification & Lingelpedia Foundation
description: Build VEGA from vanilla IronClaw to a running Lingelpedia Agent. Phase 0 verifies the environment (Docker, IronClaw, PostgreSQL, xAI API, always-on config). Phase 1 installs Neo4j, applies the Lingelpedia schema (5 node types, 15 relationship types, vector index), connects MCP servers (Neo4j, Obsidian vault, Google Calendar/Gmail/Drive), creates the Lingelpedia Agent identity file, migrates structured vault notes to Neo4j, decomposes unstructured notes into atomic claims, and validates the dual-write architecture end-to-end. Runtime: IronClaw (Rust, WASM sandboxing, MCP protocol). LLM: Grok-4.20 via xAI OpenAI-compatible API. Knowledge graph: Neo4j 5.26+ with 1536-dim vector index. Existing data: Obsidian vault (Lingelpedia) with YAML frontmatter contracts across Finance, GIX, WE, People, Properties, Auto, Family Offices domains.

userStories.title: Verify Docker and IronClaw health
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run a diagnostic script that checks Docker, IronClaw, PostgreSQL, and xAI API connectivity so that I can confirm the environment is healthy before building on it.
acceptanceCriteria: Script checks Docker is running and reports version
Script checks for orphaned/stopped containers and reports them
Script verifies IronClaw process is alive and responds to status command
Script verifies PostgreSQL is accessible (IronClaw's operational DB)
Script tests xAI API with a simple Grok-4.20 prompt and confirms response (LLM_BASE_URL=https://api.x.ai/v1, LLM_MODEL=grok-4.20)
Script checks pmset settings and warns if sleep is not disabled
Script outputs a clear PASS/FAIL summary for each check
All checks pass on the target M1 Max MacBook Pro
Create initial AGENTS.md with project overview, architecture, and environment details
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Configure always-on and Docker restart policies
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the VEGA host configured for continuous operation so that services survive reboots and the machine never sleeps.
acceptanceCriteria: pmset configured: sleep 0, disksleep 0
Docker daemon configured to start on boot (launchd or equivalent)
All Docker containers created in subsequent stories use --restart unless-stopped
Configuration is documented in AGENTS.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Install and configure Neo4j
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Neo4j running in Docker with the correct plugins and resource allocation so that the Lingelpedia knowledge graph has a storage backend. Depends on: US-001, US-002.
acceptanceCriteria: Neo4j 5.26+ community edition running in Docker container named linglepedia
Ports 7474 (HTTP) and 7687 (Bolt) exposed
APOC and Graph Data Science plugins installed
Data volume mounted at $HOME/neo4j/data for persistence
Heap allocated at 8GB (NEO4J_server_memory_heap_initial__size=8g, NEO4J_server_memory_heap_max__size=8g)
Container uses --restart unless-stopped
Neo4j Browser accessible at http://localhost:7474
Authentication configured (not default password)
Neo4j connection details documented in AGENTS.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Apply Lingelpedia Neo4j schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Lingelpedia schema (node types, relationship types, indexes, and vector index) created in Neo4j so that the knowledge graph data model is ready for data. Depends on: US-003.
acceptanceCriteria: Uniqueness constraints on all node type id fields (Claim, Entity, Source, OpenQuestion, Bet)
Vector index claim_embeddings created on Claim.embedding with 1536 dimensions and cosine similarity
Full-text index claim_content created on Claim.content
Lookup indexes created: claim_domain, claim_status, entity_type, bet_type, bet_status, open_question_status
Schema creation is idempotent (can run multiple times without error)
Schema creation script saved as a reusable Cypher file
Verification query confirms all indexes and constraints exist
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: 5 node types: Claim, Entity, Source, OpenQuestion, Bet. 15 relationship types: SOURCED_FROM, ABOUT, BELONGS_TO, RELATED_TO, CONTRADICTS, SUPPORTS, DERIVED_FROM, SUPERSEDES, TAGGED_WITH, VERIFIED_BY, STAKED_ON, RESOLVES, MENTIONS, UPDATES, PRECEDED_BY.

userStories.title: Connect Neo4j MCP server to IronClaw
userStories.description: As a developer, I want IronClaw connected to Neo4j via the official MCP server so that agents can query and write to the knowledge graph through the standard MCP protocol. Depends on: US-003.
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-neo4j-cypher MCP server installed
IronClaw MCP configuration updated to include Neo4j connection at bolt://localhost:7687
A test Cypher query (RETURN 1 AS test) executes successfully through IronClaw's MCP interface
A test node creation and deletion works through MCP (write access verified)
Connection details documented in AGENTS.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Connect Obsidian vault file system to IronClaw
userStories.description: As a developer, I want IronClaw watching the Lingelpedia Obsidian vault directory so that the Lingelpedia Agent can read vault files and write insights back. Depends on: US-001.
acceptanceCriteria: File system MCP server configured in IronClaw pointing at the Lingelpedia vault directory
Read access verified: agent can list files in vault root and subdirectories
Read access verified: agent can read contents of a .md file including YAML frontmatter
Write access verified: agent can create a new .md file in a designated _agent_insights/ directory
File watcher uses fsnotify (FSEvents on macOS) for near-instant detection
A 60-second polling fallback runs as a safety net to catch any missed events
Vault path documented in AGENTS.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up embedding pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an embedding generation pipeline so that claims can be vectorized for semantic search, contradiction detection, and the compound interest engine. Depends on: US-004.
acceptanceCriteria: Embedding model: xAI's embedding endpoint via the OpenAI-compatible API (same base URL as Grok-4.20)
A test string can be embedded and returns a 1536-dimension vector
The vector can be stored in Neo4j on a Claim node's embedding property
A vector similarity search query returns semantically similar claims
Embedding generation latency is under 500ms per claim (acceptable for Phase 1)
Pipeline is callable as a function/module that other stories can import
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Lingelpedia Agent identity file
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Lingelpedia Agent's IronClaw identity file created so that Grok-4.20 behaves as the truth engine and knowledge system defined in the Behavioral Spec. Depends on: US-001.
acceptanceCriteria: Identity file saved as lingelpedia.yaml in IronClaw's identity directory

Contains: name, role, personality (core_traits), ownership description, thinking_model (Analytical/Ambitious/Contrarian perspectives), north_star metrics, trust_level (Observe), operating_algorithm (5 steps), charter_principles, deletion_policy, and tools list
Agent instructed to read _schemas/README.md and all schema files on startup
Identity file validated against IronClaw's expected schema (no parse errors)
Agent can be instantiated with this identity file and responds in character
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Connect Google Calendar MCP
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Lingelpedia Agent to have read-only access to Mike's Google Calendar so that meeting context enriches the knowledge graph. Depends on: US-001.
acceptanceCriteria: Google Calendar MCP server installed and configured with OAuth for Mike's Google account
IronClaw MCP configuration updated to include Calendar connection
Agent can read today's calendar events through MCP
Agent can read a specific event's details (title, attendees, time, description)
Access is read-only (no write capability in Phase 1)
OAuth credentials stored securely (not in agent context window)
Connection details documented in AGENTS.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Connect Gmail MCP
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Lingelpedia Agent to have read-only access to Mike's Gmail so that email content feeds the knowledge graph capture pipeline. Depends on: US-001.
acceptanceCriteria: Gmail MCP server installed and configured with OAuth for Mike's Google account
IronClaw MCP configuration updated to include Gmail connection
Agent can search emails through MCP
Agent can read a specific email's content (sender, recipients, subject, body)
Access is read-only (no send capability in Phase 1)
OAuth credentials stored securely
Connection details documented in AGENTS.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Connect Google Drive MCP
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Lingelpedia Agent to have read access to relevant Google Drive folders so that documents feed the knowledge graph. Depends on: US-001.
acceptanceCriteria: Google Drive MCP server installed and configured with OAuth for Mike's Google account
IronClaw MCP configuration updated to include Drive connection
Access scoped to GIX, WE, and Finance folders initially (not entire Drive)
Agent can list files in scoped folders
Agent can read full document content (Google Docs text, Sheets metadata, PDF text extraction)
Large documents (>4000 tokens) chunked using sliding window approach: 2000-token segments with 200-token overlap
Agent tracks lastSyncTimestamp per folder — after initial indexing, only processes documents with modifiedTime after that mark
Access is read-only
OAuth credentials stored securely
Connection details documented in AGENTS.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build YAML frontmatter parser
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a parser that extracts YAML frontmatter from Obsidian markdown files and converts it to a structured object so that the mapper can create Neo4j nodes from vault notes. Depends on: US-006.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser reads a .md file and extracts the YAML block between --- delimiters
Parser returns a structured object with all frontmatter fields
Parser handles all template types: Entity, Account, Investment (Deal), Investment (Personal Position), Cash Flow, Person, Institution, Vehicle
Parser preserves [[wikilinks]] in body text for later relationship extraction
Parser handles missing/optional fields gracefully (no crashes on incomplete notes)
Unit tests cover each template type with representative test data
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build frontmatter-to-Neo4j mapper for Entity notes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Entity notes from the Obsidian vault to be mapped to Neo4j Entity nodes with correct properties and relationships so that legal entities are represented in the knowledge graph. Depends on: US-004, US-006, US-008, US-012.
acceptanceCriteria: Entity template frontmatter maps to (:Entity) nodes with properties: name, entity_type, legal_name, EIN, domain, aliases, tax_treatment
Ownership chains from frontmatter create (:Entity)-[:BELONGS_TO]->(:Entity) relationships
Truth score maps: verified -> 0.95, agent-populated -> 0.7, stale -> flagged for re-verification, conflicted -> creates OpenQuestion node, unscored -> 0.5
is_canonical flag preserved as node property
[[wikilinks]] to other entities create (:Entity)-[:RELATED_TO]->(:Entity) edges
A Source node is created linking back to the Obsidian file path
Mapper is idempotent: running twice on the same note does not create duplicates
Test with one real Entity note from Finance/Entities/ — verify in Neo4j Browser
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build frontmatter-to-Neo4j mapper for Account and Cash Flow notes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Account and Cash Flow notes mapped to Neo4j so that the financial account structure is in the knowledge graph. Depends on: US-004, US-006, US-008, US-012.
acceptanceCriteria: Account template maps to (:Entity {entity_type: account}) nodes with institution links, held-by references, Plaid connection status
Account -> Institution creates (:Entity)-[:RELATED_TO {type: held_at}]->(:Entity) relationships
Account -> held-by Entity creates (:Entity)-[:BELONGS_TO]->(:Entity) relationships
Cash Flow template maps to relationship edges between entities/accounts with amount, frequency, direction, tax-deductible, essential properties
Truth scores mapped per US-013 convention
Mapper is idempotent
Test with real Account and Cash Flow notes — verify in Neo4j Browser
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build frontmatter-to-Neo4j mapper for Investment and Person notes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Investment (Deal + Position) and Person notes mapped to Neo4j so that the deal pipeline and contact network are in the knowledge graph. Depends on: US-004, US-006, US-008, US-012.
acceptanceCriteria: Investment Deal template maps to (:Bet {bet_type: intentional}) nodes with cap table, cash flow history, projected IRR/MOIC
Investment Position template maps to relationship edges linking personal position to the deal's Bet node via canonical_note
Person template maps to (:Entity {entity_type: person}) nodes with relationship circle, contact frequency
Person -> company links create appropriate Entity relationships
All mappers are idempotent
Test with real Investment and Person notes — verify in Neo4j Browser
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run full structured migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all schema-driven notes in the vault (Finance, Auto, Properties) processed through the mappers and loaded into Neo4j so that the structured knowledge base is complete. Depends on: US-013, US-014, US-015.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration script discovers all schema-driven notes across Finance/, Auto/, Properties/ directories
Each note is processed through the appropriate mapper based on its template type
Wikilink cross-references between notes create Neo4j relationships
Migration logs every processed note (file path, node type created, relationship count)
No errors or skipped notes in the migration log (or errors are documented with reasons)
Neo4j Browser shows the complete entity graph with correct relationships
Count of Neo4j nodes approximately matches count of schema-driven Obsidian notes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build atomic claim decomposition engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unstructured note text (meeting notes, journal entries, operational docs) decomposed into atomic, verifiable claims stored in Neo4j so that the intelligence layer can reason over them. Depends on: US-004, US-007, US-008.
acceptanceCriteria: Engine takes a markdown file's body text (non-frontmatter content) as input
For notes over ~4000 tokens: sliding window chunking into 2000-token segments with 200-token overlap before decomposition
Each chunk gets a separate Grok-4.20 decomposition call
Claims from overlapping chunks are deduplicated by vector similarity (>0.95 cosine = likely duplicate)
Uses Grok-4.20 to extract atomic claims — each claim is a single, verifiable statement
Each claim is stored as a (:Claim) node with content, truth_score, truth_basis, domain, source_type, created_by, status
Entity extraction: people and companies mentioned in claims create/link to (:Entity) nodes
Claims link to their source via (:Claim)-[:SOURCED_FROM]->(:Source) with the original file path and raw text
Claims link to referenced entities via (:Claim)-[:ABOUT]->(:Entity)
Embeddings generated for each claim (using US-007 pipeline)
Test with 3 representative unstructured notes (1 meeting note, 1 journal entry, 1 operational doc)
Verify decomposition quality: claims are atomic (one fact per claim), verifiable, and correctly linked
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build contradiction detection via vector similarity
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the system to detect conflicting claims in the knowledge graph using vector similarity so that contradictions surface rather than hide. Depends on: US-007, US-017.
acceptanceCriteria: After a new claim is added, a similarity search finds the top-N most semantically similar existing claims
If a similar claim exists with a contradictory meaning, both claims are flagged and an (:OpenQuestion) node is created
The (:Claim)-[:CONTRADICTS]->(:Claim) relationship is created between conflicting claims
Existing conflicted truth scores from the vault create OpenQuestion nodes during migration
A report of detected contradictions is written to Obsidian as a note in _agent_insights/ using naming convention YYYY-MM-DD_contradiction_[short-description].md
Test with a deliberately conflicting pair of notes — verify contradiction is detected
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build proactive cross-domain connection engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the system to surface interesting connections between claims across different domains so that the compound interest engine starts producing value. Depends on: US-007, US-017.
acceptanceCriteria: After each claim batch is processed, a cross-domain search looks for related claims in different domains
Connection scoring: relevance based on vector similarity + shared entities + temporal proximity
Interesting connections (above threshold) are written to Obsidian as notes in _agent_insights/ using naming convention YYYY-MM-DD_connection_[short-description].md
Connection notes include: the two claims, their domains, why they are related, and a suggested action if applicable
Test: process notes from GIX/ and Finance/ domains, verify at least one cross-domain connection is surfaced
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Process remaining vault domains
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all remaining vault domains (GIX, WE, People, Family Offices) processed through the structured mapper and unstructured decomposition engine so that the full vault is in Neo4j. Depends on: US-016, US-017.
acceptanceCriteria: GIX/ notes processed: Customers, Shareholders, Meeting Notes
WE/ notes processed: Portfolio company docs, deal pipeline
People/ notes processed: Contact network
Family Offices/ notes processed: Research notes
All notes go through appropriate pipeline (structured mapper for schema-driven notes, decomposition engine for unstructured)
Migration log shows complete processing with no unhandled errors
Neo4j graph contains nodes/claims from all vault domains
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate dual-write flow — Obsidian to Neo4j
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that creating a new note in Obsidian triggers processing into Neo4j so that the capture pipeline works end-to-end. Depends on: US-009, US-010, US-011, US-017, US-018, US-019, US-020.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a new test note in Obsidian with YAML frontmatter (Entity template)
The Lingelpedia Agent detects the new file via fsnotify within seconds
The note is processed through the appropriate mapper within 60 seconds
The resulting node appears in Neo4j with correct properties
Editing the note in Obsidian triggers an update (not a duplicate) in Neo4j
Deleting processing does NOT delete the Neo4j node (Lingelpedia never deletes knowledge)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Validate dual-write flow — Neo4j insight to Obsidian
userStories.description: As a developer, I want agent insights written back to Obsidian so that Mike sees them in his normal workflow. Depends on: US-017, US-018, US-019, US-020.
acceptanceCriteria: When the Lingelpedia Agent detects a contradiction or interesting connection, it writes a note to _agent_insights/ in the Obsidian vault
The insight note uses naming convention YYYY-MM-DD_[type]_[short-description].md where type is contradiction, connection, implicit_bet, or stale_claim
Note includes YAML frontmatter with type, created_by: lingelpedia_agent, related_claims[], and suggested_action
The note body includes: timestamp, related claims with links, explanation, suggested action
The note uses proper Obsidian markdown with [[wikilinks]] to referenced entities
Mike can open the note in Obsidian and see it formatted correctly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run compound value test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that the Lingelpedia knowledge graph produces insights that would not be obvious from reading individual notes so that Phase 1's success criteria is met. Depends on: US-021, US-022.
acceptanceCriteria: Query Neo4j for implicit bets: run the currency concentration detection query from the Implementation Guide
Query Neo4j for cross-domain connections: find at least one claim from GIX/ domain that relates to a claim in Finance/ or WE/
Query for stale claims: find claims with truth scores that should be re-verified based on verification cadences
Query for unresolved open questions: list all OpenQuestion nodes with status open
Write a summary report to _agent_insights/compound_value_test.md with findings
At least one finding represents a genuine insight (not just data retrieval)
Typecheck passes
